archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Top-level CI pipeline script that orchestrates analysis and PRD validation steps; invoked directly as an executable entry point.
Service module that orchestrates MCP server registration, skill file generation, and Claude settings configuration during init.
Shared help navigation, typo correction, and keyword search utilities used across the n-dx CLI.
Pure functions for monitoring project evolution metrics and adjusting workflow parameters — no I/O or side effects.
Review gate service that collects diffs, prompts the user for approval, and handles change reversion.
Pure functions for detecting agent spin (consecutive empty turns with no tool calls).
Pure functions for counting consecutive task failures and detecting stuck tasks.
Pure functions for building a structured run summary from tool call, command, and test records.
Pure functions for analyzing run history to identify bottlenecks, failure patterns, and optimization opportunities.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting completion validation from its new location.

Orchestrates the full CLI-based agent run lifecycle — spawning Claude/Codex processes, accumulating results, handling retries, and coordinating validation/review — pure business logic orchestration with no CLI command handling or UI concerns.
Periodic heartbeat timer that writes lastActivityAt to disk during long-running agent turns.
API-provider agent loop — orchestrates Anthropic SDK calls, tool dispatch, and full run lifecycle.
Shared lifecycle helpers (brief assembly, dry-run, task transitions, review gating) reused by both API and CLI loops.
Renders task metadata (title, priority, parent chain, acceptance criteria) to the terminal before agent work begins.
Pure function for checking whether a cumulative token budget has been exceeded.
Re-exports token parsing helpers from llm-client and adds aggregate usage tracking types and functions.
Assembles a structured task brief by querying the PRD store — involves data fetching and orchestration logic.
Builds the system prompt string for the agent from project context and config.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting tool definitions and dispatch from their new location.
Command-specific help content definitions and formatter for the hench CLI.
Allowlist-based command validation with shell operator blocking and dangerous pattern detection.
Interface and type definitions for guard configuration (PolicyLimitsConfig, GuardConfig) — no logic.
Path validation utility implementing null-byte rejection, directory escape prevention, and glob-based blocked patterns.
Session-aware policy engine with stateful rate limiting, cumulative resource tracking, and an audit trail.
Explicitly documented gateway that funnels all hench→rex runtime imports through a single re-export module.
Ring-buffer metrics collector for concurrent process counts and resource utilization snapshots.
Backward-compatibility re-export shim forwarding execShell from its new location in tools/.
Re-exports all process execution primitives from @n-dx/llm-client under the familiar hench process/ path.
Process lifecycle service handling termination validation, orphan detection, automatic cleanup, and resource monitoring.
Concurrency/rate limiter — shared infrastructure with no business logic
Throttles execution based on memory pressure — infrastructure utility
Process pool management — orchestrates lifecycle of worker processes
Tracks per-process memory — low-level infrastructure helper

Schedules tasks by priority — stateful scheduling logic
Interfaces and contracts defining tool shape — no runtime logic
